prompt: Complete each sentence by changing the form of the word given in CAPITALS to fit the gap.
explanation: Each gap needs a different word form of the given root word (noun, adjective, or verb) to fit the sentence grammatically; two gaps also need a negative prefix.
passageTemplate:
1. What's the name of Dave's driving {{blank-1}}? INSTRUCT
2. Dictionaries and encyclopaedias are examples of {{blank-2}} books. REFER
3. I've only been learning Arabic for a few months, so I'm still a {{blank-3}}. BEGIN
4. In maths, you have to learn to do addition, subtraction, multiplication and {{blank-4}}. DIVIDE
5. Three of your answers were {{blank-5}}, so you got 17 out of 20. CORRECT
6. Are you really going to take part in the singing competition? I admire your {{blank-6}}! BRAVE
7. I think every child should get a good {{blank-7}}. EDUCATE
8. There's a {{blank-8}} to Albert Einstein in the town square. MEMORY
9. I want complete {{blank-9}}, so no talking at all! SILENT
10. This maths problem is too difficult for you, so I'll {{blank-10}} it a little. SIMPLE
